title: listera cordata subsp. nephrophylla
intro: Listera cordata subsp. nephrophylla, also known as Nephrophyllidion, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the orchid family, characterized by its unique leaf shape and delicate white flowers. The medicinal properties of Listera cordata subsp. nephrophylla include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits associated with this plant include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Listera cordata subsp. nephrophylla typically involve drying and processing the plant's leaves and roots to create teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
